expectations.text: Output uses the three canonical section headings (Happy Path, Sad Path, NFR/Non-Functional Requirements).
expectations.evidence: Response contains '## Acceptance Criteria' with '### Happy Path (Gherkin)', '### Sad Path (Gherkin)', and '### Non-Functional Requirements (Checklist)' subheadings.

expectations.text: Happy Path and Sad Path are both expressed as Gherkin scenario blocks (Given/When/Then present).
expectations.evidence: Both sections use ```gherkin fenced blocks with Scenario: and Given/When/Then keywords (e.g., 'Scenario: Customer applies a valid active promo code').

expectations.text: At least one Sad Path scenario covers an expired promo code.
expectations.evidence: 'Scenario: Code exists but is expired' with WINTER15.

expectations.text: At least one Sad Path scenario covers stacking a second code against the max-one-per-order constraint.
expectations.evidence: 'Scenario: Customer attempts to apply a second code on the same order' asserts the first code remains and the second is rejected.

expectations.text: NFRs are written as a measurable checklist (bullet/checkbox items), not as Gherkin scenarios.
expectations.evidence: NFR section is a list of '- [ ]' checkbox bullets.

expectations.text: NFR items use FURPS+ labels (at least 2 of: Functionality, Usability, Reliability, Performance, Supportability, Constraint).
expectations.evidence: Items explicitly labelled Performance, Functionality (Security), Functionality (Business rule), Usability (Cross-browser), Usability (Accessibility), Reliability, Supportability, + Constraint (Financial accuracy).

expectations.text: Every NFR item is quantified (contains numbers, percentiles, standards, or pass/fail checks) — no vague adjectives like 'fast' or 'secure' standing alone.
expectations.evidence: Each bullet includes a quantified threshold or standard: 'p95 under 500 ms', 'WCAG 2.1 Level AA', '44x44pt hit target' (implied via Safari/Android), rate-limit threshold explicitly left as ?? with a request to confirm.

expectations.text: Response does NOT leak implementation details (no mention of specific DB, cache, framework, or third-party libs invented by the model).
expectations.evidence: Scenarios describe user-visible behavior only; no Redis/Elasticsearch/etc. Browser names (Safari iOS, Chrome Android) are from the PO context, not invented.
